title: A Mass. youth soccer club says it sent nearly half a million dollars to a company for a European trip. Now it’s allegedly ghosting them.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The club's president says the organization is out roughly $450,000.

David Rogers / Getty Images, File A Lexington-based youth soccer club says it is out hundreds of thousands of dollars after a travel company told them it could no longer provide a long-awaited European trip just days before departure and then stopped communicating.

The Lexington United Soccer Club, a nonprofit organization, had planned to send 55 seventh- and eighth-grade players — two teams — on a trip to Scotland and London, according to club president Sean Kennedy.

title: Ken Burns speaks out on Hampshire College’s looming closure
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Education The experimental liberal arts school in Amherst is preparing to wind down operations following financial strain.
The campus of Hampshire College in Amherst on Tuesday, April 14, 2026.
Leah Willingham / AP The announcement on Tuesday that Hampshire College will close, after years of financial strain, reverberated through the campus community and among its well-known alums.

title: This Boston neighborhood is seeing the biggest Airbnb surge ahead of the World Cup
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: World Cup Airbnb booking data shows that one community in particular has already seen 122% surge in demand from fans headed to World Cup Boston.
The World Cup 2026 Countdown Clock is placed next to Faneuil Hall, in Boston, MA on Wednesday June 11, 2025.

(Pat Greenhouse/ The Boston Globe Staff) By Annie Jonas April 15, 2026 | 9:48 AM 2 minutes to read As the FIFA World Cup 2026 draws closer, early Airbnb booking data suggests some Boston-area neighborhoods are already seeing a surge in demand from traveling fans.

title: Boston activists warn of impact from Olympic policy banning trans women, intersex athletes
author: Samantha Genzer
